title: Aptitude: Trigonometry & Height and Distances - Q9

content.problemStatement: ### **Aptitude Practice Question** Angle of depression from a kite flying into the sky to a point on the ground is 45°, and it changes to 60° when the kite flies 10m higher. Find the initial height of the kite.Let the kite was flying at a height of AB initially. According to the question, in triangle ABC, we have ∠C = 45° So, AB = BC = 1/1 Then, AB = BC Now, in triangle ADC, we have ∠C = 60° So, AD/BC = √3/1 ⇒ (AB + 10)/AB = √3 ⇒ AB + 10 = AB√3 ⇒ AB(√3 – 1) = 10 m ⇒ AB = 10/(√3 – 1) = 5(√3 + 1) m The required length is 5(√3 + 1)m.
